questions.question_text: If 2, x, and 18 are in continued proportion, find the positive value of x.
A prize of ₹8,400 is to be shared between A and B in the ratio 3:5. Find B's share.
The present age of a father is thrice that of his son. After 15 years, the age of the father will be twice that of his son. What is the present age (in years) of the father?
A cylindrical water tank has a radius of 7 cm and a height of 10 cm. What is its volume? (Use $\pi = 22/7$)
The largest four-digit number which when divided by 14, 15 and 8 leaves remainder 4 in each case is?
Simplify: $\frac{1.2}{0.3} - \frac{2}{5}$
A school drum beats every 12 minutes, and the school bell rings every 18 minutes. If both sounded together at 9:00 AM, how many times will they sound together again before 1:00 AM?
Find the value of $(\frac{3}{4} + \frac{4}{3} \text{ of } \frac{1}{2} \div \frac{2}{5} - \frac{2}{4} - \frac{1}{3} \text{ of } \frac{1}{5})$.
Girish scored 448 marks in an examination and was 81 marks short of 40% of maximum marks in the same examination. His friend scored 605 marks. What is the percentage of marks scored by his friend?
The average of 6.5, 10.8, 20 and a is 15. The value of a is:

A cyclist travels from point A to B and back from B to A along the same route. On the onward journey, he covers a third of the total distance at 12 km/hr and the remaining distance at 18 km/hr. On the return journey, he covers a third of the distance at 9 km/hr and the remaining distance at 18 km/hr. Find the average speed (in km/hr) for the entire round trip.
Two trains of lengths 120 m and 180 m are moving in the same direction at speeds of 60 km/h and 40 km/h, respectively. Find the time taken by the trains to cross each other.
The sum of the digits of a two-digit number is 11. If the digits are reversed, the new number is 27 more than the original number. What is the original number?
If one angle of an isosceles triangle is $110^\circ$, what are the measures of the other angles of the triangle?

A shopkeeper offers the following schemes on toys of the same marked price.(A) Successive discounts of 20%, and 21% on any number of toys bought.(B) Successive discounts of 38%, 33% and 21% on any number of toys bought.(C) 4 toys free on the purchase of 7 toys at 27% discount on each toy thereon.(D) 3 toys free of cost on buying 4 toys.A customer wants to buy 7 toys. Which of the above schemes is the least beneficial to her?
A spherical tank has a radius of 2 meters. If the tank is filled with water to 80% of its total volume, what is the volume of water in the tank (rounded to the nearest cubic meter)?
A and B can do a piece of work in 12 days and 12 days, respectively. Both work for 3 days and then A leaves. How long (in days) will B take to complete the remaining work?

₹2,288 is divided among Pankaj, Meera, and Ashok in such a way that if ₹39, ₹47, and ₹40 are deducted from their respective shares, they have money in the ratio 17:15:14. Find Meera's share after deduction.
The simplified value of $\frac{(12 \div \frac{2}{3})}{\frac{9}{4} \times \frac{2}{6}} + \frac{18}{8} \text{ of } \frac{4}{9} \div \frac{28}{11} \text{ of } \frac{11}{9}$ is
What is the compound interest on ₹3,000 for 2 years at 20% per annum, interest compounded annually?
The following data shows the number of minutes taken by 15 students to complete a mathematics test: 45, 42, 47, 50, 44, 60, 39, 48, 52, 45, 41, 49, 46, 53. Find the median time taken by the students.
The sum of money becomes ₹7,888 after 2 years and ₹8,050.50 after 3 years on compound interest at the same rate, compounded annually. Find the sum.

A wholesaler offers two options for a sofa set marked at ₹80,000: either a flat discount of 20% or two successive discounts of 20% and 12%. What is the difference in the selling price between the two options?
A person sells two items one at a profit of 20%, and another at a loss of 10%. If both items cost ₹400 each, what is the overall gain or loss percentage?

A town had a population of 5,000 at the beginning of a year. During the first year, the population decreased by 20% and in the following year, the population increased by 20%. What was the population of the town at the end of two years?

During a sale, 50% of the goods are sold at 12% profit, 40% of the remaining goods are sold at 16% profit and the still remaining goods are sold at a loss of 19%. If there is an overall profit of 2%, then what is the value of x?

10 men and 15 women can complete a project in 12 days. If after 8 days, only $\frac{2}{3}$ of the work is completed, how many more women must be added to finish the work in the next 2 days, assuming all men continue?
Which of the following numbers is divisible by 7?

Two poles of heights 10 m and 30 m stand vertically on level ground at a distance d apart. From a point on the ground between them, along the line joining their feet, the angles of elevation to their tops of the taller and the shorter poles are $60^\circ$ and $30^\circ$, respectively. The distance d between the poles is:
The Harappan Civilization flourished mainly during which of the following archaeological eras?
What new laser-based weapon system did DRDO successfully field-test in April 2025, which is now being transferred for private sector production?

options.text: Restoration of Bengal's sovereign authority under the Mughal emperor.
Withdrawal of the Company's revenue claims from all northern subas immediately.
Granting of the Diwani rights of Bengal, Bihar, and Orissa to the Company.
Permanent transfer of Awadh territories under direct Company occupation.

A coastal plain formed by marine deposits
A marshy tract with a high water table and thick forests

options.text: It is harmless because it is quickly converted into oxygen
The incomplete combustion of fossil fuels produces it and replaces oxygen in haemoglobin
It is released only from natural forest fires and volcanoes

Permanently erases the highlighted paragraphs from existence

Workers shift from one job to another frequently.
More people are employed than actually needed, with marginal productivity being zero.

questions.question_text: Which of the following best describes 'disguised unemployment' in the Indian context?
According to the Census 2011, which of the following states was the second most populous in the country?

Seven people P1, P2, P3, P4, P5, P6 and P7 are sitting around a circular table facing towards the centre of the table. P1 sits to the immediate right of P2. P3 sits third to the left of P2. P4 is sitting immediately between P3 and P7. P5 sits immediately left of P6. What is the position of P5 with respect to P4?

Seven boxes A, B, C, D, E, F and G are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only two boxes are kept between G and E. Only three boxes are kept between G and F. B is kept immediately above A. How many boxes are kept between E and A?
What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series based on the English alphabetical order?TAV SZU PBY QXS ?
In a certain code language, 'XEQA' is coded as '49' and 'KLCH' is coded as '36'. What is the code for 'NJDX' in the given code language?

If '+' stands for '$\times$', '-' stands for '+', '$\times$' stands for '$\div$' and '$\div$' stands for '-', then what will come in place of the question mark '?' in the following equation?19 C 14 D 72 A 8 = ?

In the following number-pairs, the second number is obtained by applying certain mathematical operation to the first number. Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way are the numbers in the following sets. (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, with breaking down into numbers into their constituent digits e.g., 13 = operations on 1,3 such as adding, subtracting / multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)18, 323 19, 360

In a certain code language,A > B means 'A is the sister of B'A < B means 'A is the daughter of B'A ! B means 'A is the wife of B' andA @ B means 'A is the son of B'.How is E related to E if S > C - O = P ! E'?

Seven people P1, P2, P3, P4, P5, P6 and P7 are sitting around a circular table facing towards the centre of the table. There are exactly two people sitting between P1 and P6. P3 sits second to right of P1. P7 is sitting immediately between P5 and P6. P3 sits second to right of P4. Who is sitting immediately between P6 and P7?

Seven boxes B, C, D, E, G, R and S are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Not more than one box is kept above R. Three boxes are kept between B and R. Only one box is kept between Q and D. How many boxes are kept between S and R?

In the following triads, each group of letters is related to the subsequent one following a certain logic. Select from the given options the triad which follows the same logic.DISK - DKSI - KSID LEAF - LAFE - KAEL

Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statement(s).Statements:All cars are bars.No bar is a sun.Conclusions:(I) Some suns are bars.(II) All bars are suns.

(Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter cluster.)

Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statement(s).Statements:All cats are clocks.All bats are mats.Conclusions:(I) Some clocks are mats.(II) All mats are cats.

Yaman starts from Point A and drives 18 km towards the west. He then takes a right turn, drives 25 km, turns right and drives 24 km. He then takes a right turn and drives 28 km, takes a final left turn, drives 3 km and stops at Point P. If he follows (shortest distance and towards which direction should he drive in order to reach Point A again)? (All turns are 90-degree turns only, unless specified.)
